Part 5 — GA4 Certification Exam

Author

Mia Polito

Published

March 11, 2026

1 Purpose

Demonstrate GA4 competency by completing the official GA4 Certification Exam and reflecting on my results in a way that supports Google Analytics projects and CEP.

2 Proof of Completion

2.1 GA4 Exam Certification

3 Exam Reflection

3.1 Prompt: Results Summary: Pass/Not yet + Your Score

Response: I passed the Google Analytics 4 Certification Exam. I received an 84% passing score for the one attempt. The exam was focused on GA4 features, conversion methods, real-world applications, and metrics. The exam covered topics that were presented in the prior learning modules and sought to clarify the most useful methods to problem solving using GA4. The learning modules went into depth about how to apply the knowledge to real-world situations, which I found to be helpful not only when taking the exam but also to reference in future Google Analytics use.

3.2 Prompt: Top 3 Most Challenging Topics

  • Troubleshooting Discrepancies with Dimensions

  • Utilization of Google Ads in Google Analytics

  • Differentiating Explorations from Reports

3.3 Prompt: Two questions you wish you could redo

  1. A question that I wish I could re-attempt was based on the the Analytics 360 features. I believed I struggled with this question because I could not recall how to create new data set for a specific use and filter it. Google Analytics 360 would be a useful feature to improve efficiency when organizing data and interpreting the growth across a period of time.

  2. The second question that I wish I could redo was about troubleshooting discrepancies with by using a set of dimensions. I was unsure of which dimensions are able to detect anomalies or potential issues in Google Analytics. I believe I got stumped by this question because each dimension has multiple uses, and the usefulness of a particular dimension depends on the issue at hand.

3.4 Prompt: CEP Connection: Name one thing you will change in your CEP measurement/analysis plan because of what you learned

Response:

After learning from the Google Analytics courses and taking the exam, I can redirect this knowledge and apply my analysis for CEP. One thing that I will change in my analysis plan is to research how dimensions can help with problem-solving errors or discrepancies in my data. In doing so, I would focus on anomaly behaviors and occurrences within engagement metrics. For example, if there are unusually high conversion rates during typically slow buying periods/seasons. Further, I can also learn more about Google Analytics 360 and find out if there are developmental features that apply to future CEP projects that regular Google Analytics does not contain.

4 GA4 “readiness check” (CEP or Google Demo)

4.1 Prompt: Complete one of the following and include 1 screenshot as evidence:

4.1.1 Chosen: Option B (Google Demo):

Using the demo account, identify one KPI and one supporting exploration/report, with a screenshot.Include 2–3 sentences explaining why that KPI/report supports your project goal.

  • Identify KPI
  • Supporting Report
  • Screenshot

Average Engagement Rate

Pages and Screens Report

4.1.1.1 Explanation

I chose the Average Engagement Rate as the KPI because it measures how much time a user is spending on the website. The Pages and Screens Report helps in analyzing which specific pages are retaining the users for the longest and shortest amounts of time. By referencing both the KPI and the report, I can learn which product pages and phases of buying need improvement.

# Purpose

Demonstrate GA4 competency by completing the official GA4 Certification Exam and reflecting on my results in a way that supports Google Analytics projects and CEP.

# Proof of Completion

## GA4 Exam Certification

![Figure 1: GA4 Passing Exam](images/GA4 Completion.png)

![Figure 2: GA4 Exam Certification](images/GA4 Exam Certification.png)

# Exam Reflection

## **Prompt:** Results Summary: Pass/Not yet + Your Score

**Response**: I passed the Google Analytics 4 Certification Exam. I received an 84% passing score for the one attempt. The exam was focused on GA4 features, conversion methods, real-world applications, and metrics. The exam covered topics that were presented in the prior learning modules and sought to clarify the most useful methods to problem solving using GA4. The learning modules went into depth about how to apply the knowledge to real-world situations, which I found to be helpful not only when taking the exam but also to reference in future Google Analytics use. 


## **Prompt:** Top 3 Most Challenging Topics

-   Troubleshooting Discrepancies with Dimensions

-   Utilization of Google Ads in Google Analytics

-   Differentiating Explorations from Reports 

## **Prompt:** Two questions you wish you could redo

1.  A question that I wish I could re-attempt was based on the the Analytics 360 features. I believed I struggled with this question because I could not recall how to create new data set for a specific use and filter it. Google Analytics 360 would be a useful feature to improve efficiency when organizing data and interpreting the growth across a period of time.

2.  The second question that I wish I could redo was about troubleshooting discrepancies with by using a set of dimensions. I was unsure of which dimensions are able to detect anomalies or potential issues in Google Analytics. I believe I got stumped by this question because each dimension has multiple uses, and the usefulness of a particular dimension depends on the issue at hand. 

## **Prompt:** CEP Connection: Name one thing you will change in your CEP measurement/analysis plan because of what you learned

**Response**:

After learning from the Google Analytics courses and taking the exam, I can redirect this knowledge and apply my analysis for CEP. One thing that I will change in my analysis plan is to research how dimensions can help with problem-solving errors or discrepancies in my data. In doing so, I would focus on anomaly behaviors and occurrences within engagement metrics. For example, if there are unusually high conversion rates during typically slow buying periods/seasons. Further, I can also learn more about Google Analytics 360 and find out if there are developmental features that apply to future CEP projects that regular Google Analytics does not contain. 

# GA4 "readiness check" (CEP or Google Demo)

## **Prompt:** Complete one of the following and include 1 screenshot as evidence:

### Chosen: Option B (Google Demo):

**Using the demo account, identify one KPI and one supporting exploration/report, with a screenshot.Include 2–3 sentences explaining why that KPI/report supports your project goal.**

::: panel-tabset
## Identify KPI

**Average Engagement Rate**

## Supporting Report

`Pages and Screens Report`

## Screenshot

![Figure 2: GA4 "Readiness Check"](images/PagesScreens_Report.png)
:::

#### Explanation

I chose the ***Average Engagement Rate*** as the KPI because it measures how much time a user is spending on the website. The `Pages and Screens Report` helps in analyzing which specific pages are retaining the users for the longest and shortest amounts of time. By referencing both the KPI and the report, I can learn which product pages and phases of buying need improvement.
